name: Bernadette Soubirous (visionary)
shortDescription: Bernadette Soubirous was a French peasant girl who, in 1858, reported experiencing multiple apparitions of the Virgin Mary in Lourdes. Her visions and the subsequent healing spring at Lourdes have made her a significant figure in Catholic faith and a symbol of divine grace.
description: Bernadette Soubirous (1844-1879) was a French Roman Catholic peasant girl who is venerated as a saint. She is best known for experiencing a series of apparitions of a 'beautiful lady' in the grotto of Massabielle near Lourdes, France, in 1858. These apparitions, which occurred eighteen times, are believed by Catholics to have been the Virgin Mary. During the apparitions, Bernadette reported that the lady identified herself as the 'Immaculate Conception' and instructed Bernadette to dig in the ground, revealing a spring. The water from this spring is believed to have miraculous healing properties and has made Lourdes a major pilgrimage site for those seeking divine healing. Bernadette's unwavering faith and humble acceptance of her role, despite facing skepticism and disbelief from authorities, have cemented her position as a symbol of faith and divine grace. The Catholic Church investigated Bernadette's claims and declared the apparitions worthy of belief in 1862. Bernadette later became a nun and lived a quiet life, eventually succumbing to illness at the age of 35. She was canonized as a saint in 1933. While not a deity in the traditional sense, her role as a conduit for divine revelation and the subsequent veneration she receives elevate her to a position of significant spiritual importance, particularly within the context of Marian apparitions and pilgrimage traditions.

sources.title: Bernadette Speaks: A Life of Saint Bernadette Soubirous in Her Own Words
sources.author: Rene Laurentin

sources.title: Lourdes: Body and Spirit in the Secular Age
sources.author: Ruth Harris
